title: Khanyisa kids light up Reinet House with joy and song
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"GRAAFF-REINET NEWS - The Graaff-Reinet Museum welcomed the learners of the Khanyisa Day Care Centre on their annual visit to Reinet House on 30 September.","html":"GRAAFF-REINET NEWS - The Graaff-Reinet Museum welcomed the learners of the Khanyisa Day Care Centre on their annual visit to Reinet House on 30 September."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"“A lively group of 48 excited little ones went on a walkabout through the museum – asking questions, showing wonderful curiosity, and even entertaining us with one of their favourite songs, “Jesus is Mine”, report the museum staff."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“After the hard work of colouring in, they proudly recited the months of the year in isiXhosa – what fun it was!”","html":"“After the hard work of colouring in, they proudly recited the months of the year in isiXhosa – what fun it was!”"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The staff say that Khanyisa Day Care Centre brightened up their day.","html":"The staff say that Khanyisa Day Care Centre brightened up their day."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“We look forward to seeing them again next year, as they are annual visitors.”","html":"“We look forward to seeing them again next year, as they are annual visitors.”"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Anziske Kayster, managing director of the museum, informed Graaff-Reinet Advertiser that 12 schools visited the museum as part of the Kids in Parks programme.","html":"Anziske Kayster, managing director of the museum, informed Graaff-Reinet Advertiser that 12 schools visited the museum as part of the Kids in Parks programme."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"\"Kids in Parks\" refers to both a South African government initiative and a US-based programme designed to connect children with nature and heritage.","html":"\"Kids in Parks\" refers to both a South African government initiative and a US-based programme designed to connect children with nature and heritage."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The South African Kids in Parks Programme provides educational, three-day excursions to national parks for disadvantaged learners, focusing on biodiversity and cultural heritage.","html":"The South African Kids in Parks Programme provides educational, three-day excursions to national parks for disadvantaged learners, focusing on biodiversity and cultural heritage."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The US Kids in Parks programme offers a national network of track trails, encouraging families to explore public land and providing free adventure-based learning and prizes to promote outdoor activity and health.","html":"The US Kids in Parks programme offers a national network of track trails, encouraging families to explore public land and providing free adventure-based learning and prizes to promote outdoor activity and health."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"This programme also gives kids the opportunity to visit heritage sites and museums.","html":"This programme also gives kids the opportunity to visit heritage sites and museums."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The Graaff-Reinet Museum received visits from Thembalesizwe School (Umasizakhe), Asherville School (Graaff-Reinet), Kroonvale School (Graaff-Reinet). On 1 September, Lingcom School (Kroonvale) arrived, and on 3 September Ryneveld School (Santaville) paid a visit.","html":"The Graaff-Reinet Museum received visits from Thembalesizwe School (Umasizakhe), Asherville School (Graaff-Reinet), Kroonvale School (Graaff-Reinet). On 1 September, Lingcom School (Kroonvale) arrived, and on 3 September Ryneveld School (Santaville) paid a visit."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Narsingstreet Schoo (Asherville) came to the museum on 8 September, and on 10 September, Kamdeboo School (Aberdeen) travelled to Graaff-Reinet for its visit.","html":"Narsingstreet Schoo (Asherville) came to the museum on 8 September, and on 10 September, Kamdeboo School (Aberdeen) travelled to Graaff-Reinet for its visit."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Luxolo Primary arrived from Aberdeen on 15 September, and Isibane School from Umasizakhe on 22 September, with Aberdeen Primary having been there on 17 September.","html":"Luxolo Primary arrived from Aberdeen on 15 September, and Isibane School from Umasizakhe on 22 September, with Aberdeen Primary having been there on 17 September."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"On 29 September, Adendorp Primary enjoyed their day at the museum and on 1 October, Lettie de Klerk Primary travelled to the Graaff-Reinet Museum from Nieu-Bethesda.","html":"On 29 September, Adendorp Primary enjoyed their day at the museum and on 1 October, Lettie de Klerk Primary travelled to the Graaff-Reinet Museum from Nieu-Bethesda."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: Khanyisa kids light up Reinet House with joy and song
